definition:
1. occurs in (Homer):—; to drive out from , ἄντρου ἐξήλασε μῆλα (Odyssey by Homer); absolute to drive afield , of a shepherd, (Odyssey by Homer):—;especially to drive out  or expel from  a place, (Odyssey by Homer), etc. 
2. to drive out  horses or chariots, (Iliad by Homer): Mid. to drive out one's  horses, (Theocritus Poeta Bucolicus); so, ἐξελαύνειν στρατόν to lead out  an army, (Herdotus Historicus): hence 
3. intransitive to march out , (Herdotus Historicus): to drive  or ride out , (Thucydides) 
4. to knock out , (Odyssey by Homer) 
5. to beat out  metals, (Herdotus Historicus) (ML)
